summary: Dexamethasone stands apart from other medications due to its potential to increase intraocular pressure rather than reduce it; whereas Clonidine reduces by decreasing aqueous humor formation via adrenergic mechanisms, Mannitol lowers IOP directly by osmotically drawing fluid away, Acetazolamide decreases secretion rates effectively using inhibition techniques against specific enzymes involved in aqueous humor generation. Thus, only dexamethasone fails to meet criteria set forth regarding reduction of Intra-Ocular Pressure amongst provided choices given their distinct physiological actions impacting ocular physiology
